message:
The other day, a big storm was forecasted, and my kids were buzzing with excitement—like it was Christmas Eve! Meanwhile, I was in full prep mode, shuffling around the house, checking supplies and making sure we were ready for whatever might come our way. 

I remember my youngest asking, ‘Why are you so worried, Mom? It’s just a little rain!’ I chuckled, realizing that their carefree spirit is a gift sometimes. But I also wanted them to understand the importance of being ready. So, we turned it into a family project! We gathered our flashlights, snacks, and board games, turning our storm prep into a fun little adventure. 

It felt great to balance their excitement with a little calm preparedness. And honestly, I think we all learned something about looking out for one another, even when the skies are clear. 

Has your family ever turned a prep moment into a fun experience? Would love to hear your stories! Also, I found this little refresher helpful as we got ready.
